L1.area: JWT request routing and URL matching logic within the stream filter pipeline

L2.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists in a network proxy where URL path prefix matching used for JWT authorization does not properly honor the case-sensitivity configuration setting. When case-insensitive matching is configured, the prefix comparison still performs a case-sensitive check, allowing attackers to bypass JWT authentication by altering the letter case of URL path characters.

L3.area: JWT authentication stream filter URL prefix and path matching logic
L3.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists in the JWT authentication stream filter where URL prefix matching does not honor the case-sensitivity configuration flag. Even when case-insensitive matching is configured for a route rule, prefix comparisons are performed case-sensitively, allowing attackers to bypass JWT authentication by changing the letter case of the URL path to avoid matching the protected route prefix.

ground_truth.reason: The prefixMatcher in MOSN's JWT authentication filter ignores the case_sensitive configuration flag when matching URL path prefixes, allowing attackers to bypass JWT authorization by using differently-cased URL paths (e.g., /INDEX instead of /index).
